browserProfile.label: devio/patron-pro [my job]
browserProfile.executionHost: Windows Node via PowerShell from WSL
browserProfile.guardrail: Do not read, print, store, or commit cookies, headers, passwords, tokens, localStorage, or Google account state.

coordinateFallbacks.whenToUse: Use only when the headed browser visibly shows the expected control but the page-builder frame is exposed to CDP as an empty frame/body. Prefer selectors first.

publishButton.guardrail: Do not click until operator explicitly approves final live publish.

stateProof.correctiveAction: Clicked visible modal Save, then clicked the builder save icon. Fresh screenshot showed modal closed and Custom Code sidebar visible.

previewQa.landingFormNote: Expected false because landing_form remains empty until Twilio approval.

gotchas: The code editor can be reopened for verification after a successful save. If left open, the operator will still see a blue Save button even though the content has already persisted.

CDP can sometimes report the wrapper tab with an empty child frame while the headed Windows browser visibly shows the loaded builder UI. In that case, use a fresh screenshot and only guarded coordinate clicks for visible Save controls.
Builder save and preview proof are not the same as clicking top-level Publish. Publish remains a separate operator-approved step.
